column2: Additional updates needed for JSON<>Excel conversion script BMW HMI (for PaBi)
column3:
Dear Paul.


here's the list of checks we could see as necessary so far. I haven't added the check for intents not in source or the one for spaces before and after {} as you already added these.
Check if theres an intent name in column A in "Intent content" and a slot type name in column A in "Slot type content". Please specify if the intent name or the slot type name is missing in the warning. Any row must have content in column A.Check if there's content in columns B+C OR column D in "Intent content". If there's content in columns B+C there mustn't be content in column D. If there's content in column D, there mustn't be content in B+C.Check if there's content in columns C+D if there's content in column A in "Slot type content". There must be content in C+D for all rows in "Slot type content" for rows with content in column A.Check if there's content in column B in "Slot type content" if the content in column a starts with "BMW_".Check if all entries in column C in "Intent content" have at least one entry in column A in "Slot type content".In "Intent content" for column C entries which don't start with "BMW_", check if the content in column B is the identical in all rows where the content in column C is identical. The slot names of all non-BMW slots must be the same for all intents if they are related to the same slot type name. Provide a warning like: "Slot name and slot type name are not the same in row X as in rows Y and Z" for the row where it's different.Check if any row in "Intent content" is duplicated. This must not be the case.Check in "Intent content" if the slot names (column B) for a certain intent are added in the utterances of that intent between {} at least once.Check in "Slot type content" if an entry in column C has been added for different entries in column A. The main translation of each slot type must be different, so column C must never be the same when column A is different.Check in "Slot type content" if any entries which start with "BMW_" in the original file still have an entry in column A in the final file.


General updates:
Please make sure any content related to dummy items in the JSON structure is as in the original JSON file.Please make sure any [] "nothing"-items are the same as in the original JSON file and aren't replaced by "null".Please make it possible to delete any slot type which doesn't start with "BMW_". If these are deleted in the excel, they must be completely deleted from the JSON file, but must not be "null" or [].Please highlight the intents which must be edited in a project in the excel prepped in SFP in yellow. Please only mark column D for the other intents as "read-only" if possible as the rows themselves must be deletable to make deleting duplicates possible. If that's impossible, please don't make anything "read-only" but replace any rows belonging to intents which weren't part of the project with the original state when unprepping the file. The list of intents belonging to a project will be provided by the PM in the message to experts. 


Thanks in advance,

Romana

column2: Giesecke+Devrient Group:  plugin for GUI translations (DSI Viewer plugin)  - request for information only
column3:
Hello team!


We have finally migrated the last Mytrax customer Giesecke+Devrient to our platform.
Previously we have worked with offline Trados packages and there was one special workflow where we received an exported xliff file (origin Passolo) for translation.


The customer has sent us the following question:


Do you use the plugin for GUI translations in Trados Studio DSI Viewer? 
This would be very useful for the translators.
The DSI Viewer is particularly suitable for working with XLIFF files exported from Passolo. It shows the document structure information as well as all comments for each segment as soon as you enter the segment to be translated clicks. The plugin can easily be downloaded from the RWS AppStore.
For example:

The DSI Viewer provides you with a small viewer that you can position anywhere convenient on your screen, and this displays the DSI information and comments for each segment. Very convenient when working with DITA files, custom XML files or even SDLXLIFF files that have been exported from SDL Passolo (these contain string IDs that are very useful when translating).
If you install the DSI Viewer plugin (https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/DSI-viewer/995/ ), then go to the View tab and click on the DSI Viewer icon   a new window will appear in the Studio Editor that will display the contents of the Document Structure Information column. This window is automatically refreshed every time you navigate to a new segment.

Now my question:
Do we have something similar on the platform? 

Or if not, would it be possible to prepare the xliff, using the mentioned plugin and then translating the file in our platform?

Thank you. 
Best regards, 
Laura

column2: TM matches for Asian and RTL languages - Arjo
column3:
Hello team, 


I am writing to you because we need your help analysing a situation that we are having with our customer Arjo. 
To give you a bit of context: we translate IFU files for them - in the past Arjo created those IFU in InDesign format - nevertheless, they changed mid-last year to XML format for IFU files. 
CS team informed the customer that because of that, there was an estimated loss of about 25% against the TM. 


Some months ago, they requested a quote for many languages, IFU file on XML format. I did the quote, with the correct TM and so son, and the fuzzies were very different for some languages - see attached screenshot (1). 
Languages affected by this were: Arabic (RTL), Hebrew (RTL), Indonesian, Japanese, Thai and Vietnamese.
They complaint because this IFU was already translated in InDesign in the past (during Tstream times) for the same languages that they were requesting now on this quote. 
We thought that there was some missing content because of the migration from Tstream to LW so we compensate the customer because of that and re-sent the quote by manually changing the wordcount so all languages +/- were the same.
Link to the project: 90829-76950004-100190875 Arjo Slide sheets IFU rev 4 - PO 2903741


Some days ago, they requested the same IFU quote but now only for Chinese as target. We did the OW, word analysis and so on as always, and the customer complaint again on the quote price because of the low fuzzy matches. 
This IFU was translated in the past (LW platform) in InDesign, so they were expecting more fuzzy matches as explained about (only estimated loss of about 25% against the TM). 

The customer sent this screenshot (2) comparing the files and the TM leverage. 
Attached you can also see the screenshot of the Chinese wordcount (3). 
Link to the project: 90829-76950004-100190875 Arjo Slide sheets IFU rev 4 CN reg - PO 2922694


We are reaching out to you because, this can't be related to the TM migration from Tstream to LanguageWire platform as all languages (equally) would have been affected. Therefore, as this issue is only affecting Asian languages and RTL languages, our assumption is that it may be related to that. It may be something related to the segmentation of XML files for Asian and RTL languages? 


I am sending you examples of the XML files so you can check. We really need to give an explanation to the customer since they are a bit relunctant of paying - I hope you can help us :)


Thanks!

column2: The Binding Site - soft lock segments during SFP
column3:
Hello Victor, 
 María asked me to reach out to see if you can assist with
our client The
Binding Site.
 To give you more context: 
We've received frequent feedback that translators aren’t
updating the TM when needed and aren’t thoroughly reviewing CMs and 100%
matches, leading to errors in our translation memory and making mistakes in the
deliveries to the client. To address this, we made some changes to avoid
receiving more feedback:
 We created a spreadsheet with common and recurring terms
that form a structural base for the files. These terms were translated and
validated by the client.
 These are the actions taken so far:

Cascading
     TM: is
     not suitable mainly because of the pricing element (i.e. we downgrade the
     content of the secondary TM family, meaning that the customer would now
     need to pay for it and we can’t choose this option).
Transformers
     or processors: Tomas Lubusky and Mikkel Madsen found this unfeasible,
     as analysis against the spreadsheet, TB or TM is not straightforward, also
     transformation hard locks the content and excludes it from the word count
     so definitely not suitable. 
SFP
     done by engineers:
     Short-term however, probably the only solution would be project by project
     file preparation step done by your team.


Since these terms aren’t non-translatables, soft-locking
non-translatables won’t work.
While they often appear as CMs and 100% matches, we can’t
use platform settings to lock them entirely, as we still need translators to
review all the other CMs and 100% matches that appear in the files.
 Could you confirm if this is something your team can handle
during a SFP job? If so:

Can you provide the cost
     for SFP and the estimated turnaround time once received?
Can you provide the cost
     for TFC and the estimated turnaround time once received?


I've attached the spreadsheet with the terms that need to
be soft-locked and two source files for reference from project INS070.U.OPT
  INSQ070.U.OPT IVDR 2910620. 
We always receive .docx from the client and we send them to
pre-dtp. You would receive the SFP job after pre-dtp and we normally translate
2 files per project.
 Let me know if you need more details or if you'd like to
discuss this over a call.
 Thank you!
 Best,
Sara

column2: Support for Thuasne Projects / Change in Workflow - Source File Prep & Target creation
column3:
Hi team,
I’m reaching out to you as we are looking to optimize our
workflow for Thuasne, one of our pharmaceutical customers.
This customer sends us Studio packages, with several
languages, and we need to deliver return packages (+ eventually target files).
We typically receive 1 to 5, sometimes 8, Studio packages at
a time, each with 1 to several documents to translate. These documents are
often partly or almost completely translated, contain locked and validated
segments, which we should not change but must be visible to translators. TM
included in the package must be used in the translation workflow. Source language is FR, and we typically have 8 to 18 target languages.Source file prep should result in files that can be handled
within SE, in a TR – PR of TR – PR implementation workflow, with TM received in the

column2: Question - how to identify the word count details when a Tra 2.0/PEMT job is interrupted in the middle of progress
column3:
Dear colleague,

I would like to get your advice on this question:

We sometimes encounter such a case where our customer needs to stop our translator's process for Tra 2.0/PEMT in the middle of progress, due to various reasons. For example, our translator has been working on a job for 50 segments in total, and the customer says "Please stop the process!" when the translator has translated until the segment 36. We then needs to know about word count specifically for the texts between segments 1 to 36. The question is how to calculate that.

What would be the quickest way for a PM to get the word count for specific segments? Would that require some easy step by the Engineer team?  (Could they check it in the server if a PM specifies the start- and end segment numbers?)

If it's nothing Engineer team can just check and retrieve from the system smoothly: would it then require an extra step for example like (1) PM contacts Engineer team and specify the segments for which the team needs to know about word count details, (2) Engineer somehow preps the source document based on the segment numbers (like "locking" the segments that are not post-edited by the translator and have to be excluded from word count), (3) and re-run a new word count analysis on the prepped document?

Please advise me what way would be the most efficient way for a PM to get a new word count only for the specific segments.

Thank you for your support 🙏

Best regards,

Shino Sakaki Gunnfors

Sophos Limited always sends us Trados packages (zip) exported from their SDL World Server. We therefore start each new project with Source File Prep to ask Engineer team to create XML for External wordcount analysis (and Excel WC report).

Due to some difference  in the word count logic between SDL system and our own, our PM team always need to manually move the number counted as "New words" to the fuzzy category "75 - 84%" (so, "New words" should be changed to zero, and the words from "New words" need to be added to "75-84%").

This is a manual step to fix once a WC analysis is done and caused several errors due to human factors (missing to manually change those words, typing a wrong number in the WC field which led to wrong customer- and vendor prices, the changes were not saved in the field when the system performance was poor etc. etc.)

Sophos is trying to use more and more PEMT projects and the extra work load for PMs and the risk of errors will be increasing.

Ideally, once we send a SFP to Engineer from the project created from this template: Project Template Name 170281: Sophos MT Post Editing, I want to get a XML which automatically moves new words to "75-84%".

Would it be possible to get this solution?

Thank you.

column2: Weber NL update - missing spaces - filter segments
column3:
Hello team
I'm wondering if you might have a suggestion for a solution to my problem.


We have a very big Weber project (30.000+ words)

https://platform.languagewire.com/ProjectBox/3002039
NL assignment: https://platform.languagewire.com/Order/9873665


The customer has now let us know that after importing the content to the site, they see quite a few instances where a space is missing between two words.
We can see that the translator probably missed the space because it's in combination with a tag.


For example:

EN: [1]Precise, consistent heat[1] gets to temp quickly and cooks food evenly across the grates for perfectly grilled meals every time.
NL: De[1]nauwkeurige, gelijkmatige warmte[1] bereikt snel de juiste temperatuur en grilt het voedsel gelijkmatig over de grillroosters voor perfect gegrilde maaltijden, elke keer weer.

This should have been:
NL: De [1]nauwkeurige, gelijkmatige warmte[1] bereikt snel de juiste temperatuur en grilt het voedsel gelijkmatig over de grillroosters voor perfect gegrilde maaltijden, elke keer weer.


I've tried looking up the segments in TMMS. I filtered on the segments from this project, and tried getting only segments with tags, but that's not possible (yet) in TMMS.


Another way to fix this would be to make a new copy of the whole NL job, and send it to the translator and ask her to check all spacing around tags.


This very much seems to be only a problem in segments with tags, and only in some of them. 


The customer is fixing this on their side, so, as far as I know, we don't need to redeliver this, but, this is a kind of project which will likely come back next year with much of the same content, so it would be very good to have this fixed in the TM, so we don't automatically repeat the same mistakes next year.


Is there anything you can do to help make it easier to fix this in the TM?
Is it possible to create a file or job which only has segments which include tags? Or is it even possibly to narrow it down further and find exactly the segments which have these problems?


Any input you might have on how to fix this will be most welcome :)


Best,

Katrine

column2: ATLAS COPCO, Request of feasibility: File preparation to convert anything below 94% to no matches
column3:
Hi Victor,

We have a request of file preparation
feasibility for the engineering team, to solve a challenging case with our enterprise customer
Atlas Copco. Please note that this request
is only to investigate the feasibility, nothing more at this stage as we are still
deciding what is the best way forward.

Current situation: 
Atlas copco orders a mix of
human translation and machine translations+validation via AEM connector. They
have cascading TMs, with TMs that were imported from RWS. These TMs have a mix
of paragraphs sentence based structure, for which Paul Bijnens created a
Transformer to increase the TM matches. 
Current process --  We receive via AEM connector UDF files   files are
transformed automatically via Atlas Copco/UDF transformer to adapt segmentation
and convert UDF into tsxlf   tsxlf are translated and validated   transformer
converts to UDF again   delivery of translations in AEM via connector



Issue: 
our pre-translation in SE works as follows: anything
above 75% match is taken from the cascading TM sets, anything below 74% is
Machine-translated without looking at the TMs. 
Due to several feedbacks
about the TM matches and low quality in the high fuzzies, we are thinking to
provide to atlas copco only matches from 95%-100% and context matches, anything
below should be translated with MT only.
This is currently not
possible automatically, Devs are developing a functionality to adjust TM threshold
on a project base, for late Q3. We cannot wait until then since we are in a
critical spot with the customer, therefore investigating this workaround.



Question: is it feasible for engineers to prepare the tsxlf
file, mark anything below 94% as a No match so we can upload it in the platform
and translate:

column2: Atlas Copco Request for solution evaluation
column3:
Hi Team,


here is the ticket to track time spent so far for evaluation of a switch to sentence based TM structure

Below is the latest reply from Víctor
25 hours have been spent so far from the engineers, 


Hi all!
Below my findings after some tests. I've resegmented the AC Edwars Legacy TMs which are the smaller ones for the tests and the results (technical-wise) are very good. The TMs are around 60k words in size and only around 150 words are to be reviewed so pretty better than what I expected (even in Korean!).
What I did:Converted the TMX into bilingual xlf files resegmenting both source and target using srx segmentation rules matching those for file prep (important we're not breaking at colon and that should be replicated in the Transformer for consistency)Fixing an adding segmentation rules to match full sentencesDeveloped a python script to remove the content from target tags whenever the number of mrk tags (aka segments) wasn't matching those in the corresponding sourceDeveloped a python script to mark correct segmented units as LOCKED, so leaving editable only wrong segment matching unitsPretranslated the empty segments with a low fuzzy threshold to fill those segments with content to fix
I've created a sample project on the Platform so we can see how it would look for vendors to fix, here you can check it as well:
Korean: Smart Editor | LanguageWire
Czech: Smart Editor | LanguageWire
Spanish: Smart Editor | LanguageWire
German: Smart Editor | LanguageWire
And attached are the full xlf files (these, once the fix is done in the platform, will be converted into TMX segmented by sentence).
To take into account:
Again, this is a semiautomated approach, meaning we are slicing text based on rules, being the text marketing translation, the translation is creative and the order of the sentences in the same paragraph may be inverted in the target language, an example of this can be seen here:
 This leads me to strongly advise to add a penalty to the TMs of at least a 1% to make sure everything is reviewed and validated and to avoid wrong content to be 100%+ matched while it's not correct.
As an idea, if the Platform doesn't allow us to add a 1% penalty, we can add a random character to the source so we make sure none of them are 100%+ match and they become 100%+ matches once they're reviewed and approved by the vendorsAlso, these are the smaller TMs shared for testing and the time invested by my team has been considerable (of course we won't invest the time to develop any other script as it's done, but other issues with bigger TMs may arise when proceeding) so we need to make sure the customer agrees on the process and knows the engineering cost of it.

1. Password
File   Properties   Select "Security" tab   Select "Password Security"


In new window: "Permissions" section, select "Restrict editing and printing..." and then: 
Printing Allowed: "High Resolution", Changes Allowed: "Commenting, filling in form fields, and..."Unticked: "Enable copying of text, images, and..."Ticked: "Enable text access for screen reader devices for the visually impaired"Change Permissions Password: cross555Click "OK" and enter the password again.





2. Language setting 


File   Properties   Select "Advanced" tab   Section "Reading Options:
Binding: Left EdgeLanguage: depending on the language code in the file name

de-DE: German

fr-FR: French

nl-NL: Dutch

pt-BR: Portuguese Brazil







Please let me know if everything is clear and if you could help us creating a script that would automate this process.


Thank you,
Szymon

column2: (VERY URGENT) request on solution to create XML for External WC analysis based on WC analysis document downloaded from client's Phrase TMS
column3:
Dear engineer team,

I have a very important request that needs your response and support very urgently:

Entity: Sophos Limited

For this Sophos Limited, which sends source materials from their RWS WorldServer system, we currently use the EWC method with your help to create XMLs for WC analysis based on the source Trados packages uploaded by Sophos requestor to each project (please see: https://languagewire.cloud.xwiki.com/xwiki/bin/view/Delivery/Engineering/Customer%20instructions/Sophos%20Limited/).

Sophos decided to change their TMS from the RWS WS to Phrase (Phrase TMS – Phrase). In the new work process we need to implement, Sophos team will create projects ONLY on Phrase, not on the LW platform any longer, and our translators need to translate directly on the Phrase CAT tool, by logging into the system. 

Since invoicing to the client and payment to the vendors need to be done from our platform, our PMs will need to duplicate each project in our platform and fill in the word count information by downloading WC from Phrase. Based on the amount of projects and target languages, letting PMs manually copy and paste each word counts would generate too much extra workload and too high risk of human errors.

With this request, I would like to ask you to check how your team could create XML files you now create for EWC based on the WC analysis files downloaded from Phrase system.

(Please note: I had already sent a request to Productboad to get a solution to be able to upload Phrase WC analysis file in CSV directly to the EWC "Add Files..."-field.


Taras Mordvinov analyzed the samples of the downloaded WC analysis files from Phrase (in format CSV, CSV with chars, LOG, JASON -  we can choose one of them), and Taras gave me this comment as his analysis:

"I did my research on the files and my conclusion is that manual transformation is unrealistic approach. As this is money - validation is very strict and won't let in files that are not exactly following the expected pattern. And the structure is way to different, columns need to be moved, deleted, added, renamed. On top of that files are computer generated and neither Excel nor Libreoffice Calc can handle it very well and I ended up modifying it as plain text. Otherwise I could not open it or it got corrupted after saving. So unfortunately I can't see how can it be possible to do such modifications on a large scale"  )

Based on Taras' message, I understand he find it too difficult to create a solution enabling our PMs just to put a downloaded WC file in CSV from Phrase to our EWC and get the figures filled in, but I would like to ask you to check if you could, instead, create a WC XML file as you do for the Trados packages now, based on Phrase WC files in CSV (or LOG, or JASON).

This automation is extremely important for PMs' workload to be kept at a manageable level and also to ensure that our customer- and vendor prices are calculated based on correct word count.

I will send you some sample files here.

Please note: they will stop using the RWS WS at the end of October and we need to implement this new process ASAP, preferably within 2 weeks.

Could you please start to check and let me know about the approximate time your team will need for this solution ASAP, by Tuesday the 14th October? I will need to share your assessment with CX leaders to discuss the PM resources needed for the new Sophos-Phrase workprocess.

I appreciate your support.

Best regards,

Shino Sakaki Gunnfors

The customer sent for quote 415 files (original FrameMaker files and the MIF files to be used for translation) : see link PONE_CUG_Product_65736M_ForPT.zip.


 We asked for a merged mif file to be created for all included MIF files.  When we tried to perform a word analysis on this merged file, it failed. We performed a word analysis with Trados Studio, which reported 158 149 words for translation (120 274 new words + 37875 repetitions).

=  Could you help to explain why this word analysis failed ? It it because of the filesize (10 MB) ?

=  We would like to assign 3 translators + 1 reviewer to this project (if accepted by the customer): as a consequence, could you prepare the merged file and split it into 3 files of around 40 000 words each ?


For information, Trados Studio automatically included the needed text insets into the translation scope - only the main MIF files (126 without the book and TOC) were used in the wordcount performed in Trados Studio.

=  Could you confirm whether the text insets (290 MIF files) need to be included or not in the merged file for word analysis and translation ?


In order to confirm our word analysis in Trados Studio, we would like to analyse the SDLXLIFF files we created in the platform : see zip here Trados Studio_sdlxliff source.zip. Last Friday, our try to perform a word analysis in the platform failed.

=  Could you help us to perform a word analysis on the attached SDLXLIFF files ? Which settings should we use in the platform ?

=  Do the SDLXIFF files need to go through a transformer to be analyzed ?


In advance, thank you for your prompt reply and help. We urgently need to know whether those files can be processed in the platform.




Kind regards,
Hélène

column2: Daimler Trucks: Adaptation request for browser-based Pixel Constraints QA tool to accomodate row width and maxlines information
column3:
Dear team,


I discussed with Víctor (VIPA) that we have the following project type for Daimler Trucks: 


Example project: Daimler Buses Order MY3 Part 3 (WDB) 3476416, One source ZIP attached.


Characteristics:
We get XMLs in ZIP files like the one attached as source.We get row width information in the XMLs like: 

We get information regarding the max amount of lines allowed for each segment: 



At the moment, the "Daimler/LanguagePackage" transformer shows the row width through the QA checker "Verify text dimensions" as the length restriction in Smart Editor one time. The transformer also adds line breaks in the source and target fields based on the max number of lines allowed, but doesn't provide the row width length restriction per line or as many times as there are lines, it just applies it once. I've tried to get other options in Smart Editor through Ticket/276086, but haven't gotten a time line for changes from the transformer team. 


That's why I'd like to request the following from LE:
Could you adapt the browser-based Pixel Constraints QA tool to reflect the row width and the maximum number of lines allowed?Could we add Advanced File Preparation (AFP) + QA check + Target File Creation (TFC) jobs to our workflow after PEMT and PoT to get the HTML files for a QA check by the vendors? AFP + TFC would be for engineers, the QA check job would be for the vendor to perform the check.
Víctor mentioned that development like this one would need to be monetized. That's why I'm adding the CSM (MAJA) in this ticket as I'm pretty sure the customer already paid for the transformer creation and thinks that we check the row width information per line for as many lines as the XML allows. Could you confirm that, @Marie-Christine? If the customer already paid for the transformer development, could we still get this development, @Vïctor?


Based on the answers in this ticket, I'd update the one for the transformer team.


Thanks in advance,

Romana

column2: Fresenius- environment for dita files from ixiaccms
column3:
Hello
we have received the first project of the Exelia line of business from Fresenius. This BU was localized by Lionbridge until now
If everything goes well with this first project we will get more languages and more documents for localization.
It is also the first set of files that has been exported from IXIACCMS. 
There are 733 files in total
Client needs also the augmented review. 


First of all : Fresenius projects always contain more than 400 dita files. It would be great if it was possible to easily upload and process this amount of files in the platform
then
The Dita files need to be compiled for the augmented review after translation. So far we have used  the AUGMENTED REVIEW/DITA transformer and we could  compile the files for the augmented review without problems
I think that a new transformer for the new type of file is needed 


I have created the project https://platform.languagewire.com/ProjectBox/3675022 and Emmanuel Cinneri started working on the advanced file preparation. He then suggested to open a ticket because a new environment for Augmented Review is needed for the new set of files exported from the new Fresenius CMS.


In the Internal Project Files of project 3675022 you will find the Plugin and DTD provided by madcap to Fresenius : com.fresenius.dita13.dtd.zip
While in the Project Reference files you will find the full package of DITA and images that we received from the client : jbf1764683157720_00005_1771919584589.lmanifest.zip 
(I could not attach this file to this ticket because the zip is too big)


Please note that a special treatment is needed for the file ifj1770908424201.dita: it has to be translated with the Exelia UI translation memory in priority. I created a different assignment only for this file BUT we will need to insert it in the package for the augmented review, together with all the other files of the DOC. Please advice if a different organization of the assignments is needed.


For your info, we charge the client for the advanced file preparation, for the target file preparation and for the augmented review (0.010 per word, we charge for the total wordcount, for each language)
Please do not hesitate to let me know if you need more information
thank you
Paola

We have a new customer for Mettler Toledo who will be sending xml files for translation. They have sent us two files for test of the same project, one only for new content, one with legacy translations. They were hoping we could have the already translated parts in our tool for context. Could you please advice if this is possible? I see with the transformer we now have for other Mettler projects with xml files the copy taken for translation is exactly the same from both files.


Would there be a possibility to also have the translated parts as locked segments in SE for reference?


You can see two assignments here, one for each file mentioned.
